description_long: This trumpet features a striking steampunk-inspired design, with a metallic body adorned in bronze, copper, and brass tones. The bell is wide and flared, with a deep reddish-copper finish that catches the light. Along the main tube, several mechanical elements such as gears, coils, and exposed wiring add a complex, industrial aesthetic. The valve section is robust, with three piston-style valves positioned centrally and accented by metallic and rubberized details. The mouthpiece is clearly visible, attached to a slightly curved leadpipe that connects to the main body. Overall, the instrument combines traditional trumpet form with imaginative, mechanical embellishments.
description: A steampunk-style trumpet with a wide, flared bell, metallic body, and visible gears and wiring. The design blends classic brass instrument features with industrial, mechanical details.
description_view_0: From this end-on view, the mouthpiece and the cylindrical body are prominent, with mechanical details and a gauge visible on the side.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the full length of the trumpet, showing the flared bell, three piston valves, and intricate gears and wiring along the body.
description_view_2: Looking directly into the bell, the wide, copper-colored flare dominates, with the rest of the body receding behind it.
description_view_3: The opposite side view reveals more of the mechanical components, including coiled wires and additional metallic fixtures, as well as the valve arrangement.
attribute_bell_flare: The bell is wide and dramatically flared, with a gradual outward curve and a deep, open mouth.
attribute_valve_configuration: Three piston valves are arranged in a straight line along the top center of the instrument, each with a visible button and mechanical detailing.
attribute_leadpipe_curvature: The leadpipe is gently curved, connecting the mouthpiece to the main body with a smooth, flowing bend.
attribute_tuning_slide_location: The main tuning slide is located just after the leadpipe, before the valve section, and is U-shaped, oriented horizontally.
